description:
You are a 3-rd line support engineer who has just received a tarball of “raw” diagnostics from a customer.  
Everything has already been unpacked for you under  

  /home/user/diag/raw/  

Your manager wants a single, **column-aligned CSV** that marketing can import into a spreadsheet.  
Because the marketing people have zero Linux skills, the CSV _must_ be exactly in the format described below, and it _must_ be produced **only** with standard GNU text utilities (cut, paste, tr, sort, grep, etc. — no Python, Perl, or Ruby).

OBJECTIVE  
Create the file  

  /home/user/diag/summary/diag_report.csv  

with **one header line followed by exactly one data line**.  
The file must look **exactly** like this (including commas, capitalisation, order of columns and the final trailing newline):

MemTotal_kB,MemFree_kB,MemAvailable_kB,Root_Size_GB,Root_Used_GB,Root_Avail_GB,Var_Size_GB,Var_Used_GB,Var_Avail_GB
8173400,204800,3500000,40G,20G,18G,80G,60G,18G

SOURCE DATA  
1. /home/user/diag/raw/memory_stat.log – excerpt from /proc/meminfo  
2. /home/user/diag/raw/disk_stat.log   – excerpt from “df -h”  

The files already exist; do **not** rename or edit them directly.  
You must:

1. Extract the second numeric column (ignore the trailing “kB”) from the lines  
   MemTotal, MemFree and MemAvailable in memory_stat.log.
2. From disk_stat.log, pull the Size, Used and Avail columns for  
   a) the line whose “Filesystem” is “/dev/sda1” (mounted on “/”)  
   b) the line whose “Filesystem” is “/dev/sda2” (mounted on “/var”)  
   Preserve the capital “G” suffix on each number.
3. Assemble the nine individual values into the single CSV line shown above, in the exact order used in the header.
4. Place the header on the first line and your comma-separated data on the second line.
5. Finish your work in **no more than 5–10 shell commands** (the exact number is not graded, but that gives you an idea of the expected complexity).

COMMAND LOG  
Create a second file  

  /home/user/diag/summary/diag_commands.log  

that logs every command you actually executed to build the CSV.  
For each command write a line in the following exact format (one line per command):

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS  <the shell command you typed>

Use the 24-hour ISO timestamp produced by  
  date +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"  
Put the lines in the same order you executed the commands.

PERMISSIONS & LOCATION  
• All work must be done inside /home/user (you already own it, no sudo required).  
• If any temporary working files are needed, keep them in /home/user/diag/working/.

DELIVERABLES (checked by the automatic grader)  
1. /home/user/diag/summary/diag_report.csv exists and **exactly** matches the two-line sample shown above.  
2. /home/user/diag/summary/diag_commands.log exists, has at least 5 lines, and every line matches the timestamp + command format.  

No additional output is required; the grader will cat the two files to verify their contents.
truth:
Initial filesystem that is already present when the task begins:

/home/user/diag/raw/memory_stat.log
-------------------------------------------------
MemTotal:       8173400 kB
MemFree:        204800 kB
MemAvailable:   3500000 kB
Buffers:        512000 kB
Cached:         1024000 kB
-------------------------------------------------

/home/user/diag/raw/disk_stat.log
-------------------------------------------------
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted_on
/dev/sda1        40G   20G   18G  55% /
/dev/sda2        80G   60G   18G  77% /var
tmpfs           3.9G     0  3.9G   0% /run/user/1000
-------------------------------------------------

Pre-created writable directories:
/home/user/diag/summary/      (empty)
/home/user/diag/working/      (empty)

Files the agent must produce:

1. /home/user/diag/summary/diag_report.csv
Exact required content (two lines, Unix LF line endings):

MemTotal_kB,MemFree_kB,MemAvailable_kB,Root_Size_GB,Root_Used_GB,Root_Avail_GB,Var_Size_GB,Var_Used_GB,Var_Avail_GB
8173400,204800,3500000,40G,20G,18G,80G,60G,18G

2. /home/user/diag/summary/diag_commands.log
Must contain ≥5 lines; each line must match regular expression:
^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}  .+

No other files are required but the presence of extraneous files will not fail the test, provided the two deliverables are correct.
